The first thing you're going to do is you're going to need to research. Research is the most important step. It's what makes or breaks the rest of the steps. If you don't do your research right, then you're doing all the other steps at a waste. If you do your research right, you do all the other steps, you can have dominating results. Make sure you do the research. The first part of the research, you're going to start at google.com. What you're going to do is you're going to look for the Google AdWords tool. I'm just going to type in AdWords tool. Make sure after you've gone to this website, you're going to want to bookmark it. I go to the AdWords keyword tool. What this keyword tool is going to let me do, let's say Oprah talked about a face lifting product. We're going to do facelift. Then we're going to do hvplq. Normally, we would be typing in the actual product name. I'm going to go ahead and hit get keyword ideas. The tool takes a little while. Then it's going to spit out all the different suggestions and some information about these keywords. It's saying that people are typing in facelift, mini facelift, all these other keywords here. Then we see over on the left and right hand columns, we see the recent month's search volume and the average monthly search volume. This column here tells us the advertiser competition, which means how many people are paying for ads. The more people that are paying for ads means that chances are people are buying on this term and that's why they're willing to pay for ads. What we're going to be looking for here is something that's got a decent search number. Then we're going to take that keyword. Let's say I'm just going with one here, one hour facelift. I'm going to right click and choose copy. Now I'm going to go back to Google and I'm going to find another tool. This tool is called the keyword difficulty tool. I'm going to hit search. The one that I want to use is the one that's by seologs.com. It's my favorite one. It's very easy to use. I'm just going to click on that listing and I recommend you bookmark this tool also. You'll be using it a lot. I'm going to open up another tab and I want to go back to Google again. This time I want to find out how much competition is on this keyword. What I'm going to do is I'm going to take that keyword that I copied out of the Google AdWords tool. I'm going to put some quotation marks around my keyword and I'm going to hit Google search. Now I've typed this one hour facelift in a Google with quotation marks and over here on the right hand side of my screen, it tells me what the results are. This tells me that there's only 1400 competing pages. I'd like to see anything under 10,000 so 1400 is very, very good. I'm liking the signs of this already. Now I'm going to go back to the Google AdWords tool and see how many visitors a month that was getting since I see that that was a good keyword. Where was that keyword? I think it was up. Here keyword. There it is. One hour facelift. 320 searches per month are searching this phrase and it looks like we can dominate it. Now I'm going to use the keyword difficulty tool and this tool is going to really let me know yes or no can I really dominate this keyword. It looks like I can just by looking into Google. Now I'm going to paste that keyword into this keyword difficulty tool and then I'm going to click submit and I'm looking for any number under 50. 36. 36 is I love to see 36. My favorite is anything under 30, under 35, 33, 30, 21. Those are my favorite. Those are just candy from a baby. But 36 is easy also. Using my methods you should be able to get anywhere from three to seven top listings and probably a couple, several second and third page listings as well. So you should really do well on this one. So we found a keyword and that was as easy as we did. I just went in there. I guessed on one. I just took a chance with it and I said, you know, let's try this one. It looks good. One hour facelift. The reason I chose that one is because it's very specific. I know what that person wants. If they're typing into the Google search engine, one hour facelift, I know what they want. They want a one hour facelift. So that means I can create a landing page or I can write an article that is about a one hour facelift. I can point them to an affiliate product that gives them the benefits of a one hour facelift. And, you know, because I know exactly what they want when they're typing in that keyword, I can tailor the offer to their specific needs and that's when sales happen.
